notes: Gene-specific rescue-literature dossier for Quiver's 13 validated TSC2-KO modifier predictions. Ranked by mechanistic proximity to the TSC2-RHEB-mTORC1 axis: Tier 1 (CDK9, ACTR3) direct mTOR-complex / lysosomal-tethering evidence; Tier 2 (BCL2, KMT2D, VPS54, FZD7) solid indirect/coherent rescue mechanism; Tier 3 (RPS3, SMARCE1, DPM2, NCOA6) weak/speculative; Tier 4 (SSU72, DIDO1, SAP18) no mTOR/autophagy/TSC evidence. Notable: CDK9 (moat opposite-rank #42) and ACTR3 (not in moat top-50) are the strongest literature hits but weak/absent moat signal — the literature re-prioritises Quiver's set.

evidence.claim: CDK9 (TIER 1, strongest): a direct physical binding partner of the mTOR scaffold mLST8, forming distinct CDK9-mTOR complexes (nuclear CTORC1 with RAPTOR, cytoplasmic CTORC2 with RICTOR); CDK9 inhibition phenocopies mTOR-complex disruption and CDK9 knockdown regulates mTOR/SREBF1/BCL2. CDK9 loss could suppress TSC2-KO via disrupting CTORC1 independently of the TSC2-RHEB axis. Also blocks PINK1-PRKN mitophagy (SIRT1-FOXO3-BNIP3).
evidence.source: Beauchamp 2019 Blood; Borden 2019; Yao 2022 Autophagy; Zhu 2025 Cancer Biol Ther

evidence.claim: ACTR3/Arp3 (TIER 1): a structural subunit of the Arp2/3 complex that builds the branched endolysosomal actin microdomains required to recruit RAGC and mTOR to the lysosomal surface; inhibiting actin polymerisation or silencing WASH/Arp2/3 impairs mTORC1 activity. In TSC2-KO (mTORC1 locked to the lysosome via RHEB-GTP), destabilising this actin scaffold could suppress hyperactivation. NOTE: not in the moat top-50 — a literature-only hit.
evidence.source: Priya 2023 Sci Adv; Li 2023 Cell Tissue Res; Simonetti 2019

evidence.claim: BCL2 (TIER 2): the canonical autophagy gatekeeper — sequesters Beclin-1 (BECN1) at its BH3 domain, blocking PI3KC3/autophagosome nucleation; BCL2 loss releases BECN1 and restores autophagic flux that mTORC1 suppresses in TSC2-KO (a bypass rescue, not direct mTOR suppression). TET2 promotes autophagy by suppressing BCL2-BECN1.
evidence.source: Devis-Jauregui 2021 Autophagy; Kumariya 2021 Autophagy; Yang 2022 Autophagy

evidence.claim: KMT2D (TIER 2): KMT2D directly activates DDIT4/REDD1 (a negative mTORC1 regulator that competes with RHEB for TSC2) via H3K4 methylation, and upregulates SMG1 to antagonise AKT-mTOR; KMT2D-deficient cells show mTOR-driven ribosome biogenesis. Context-dependent (LOF activates mTOR → the rescue is likely KMT2D RE-EXPRESSION/GOF), which would suppress mTORC1 independent of TSC2.
evidence.source: Xu 2023 Adv Sci; Sun 2025 J Leukoc Biol

evidence.claim: VPS54 (TIER 2): the complex-specific GARP subunit governing endosome->TGN retrograde trafficking; its loss depletes lysosomes of hydrolase machinery and disrupts lysosomal identity (sterol accumulation), impairing the lysosomal platform mTORC1 needs for nutrient-sensing activation — an indirect, organelle-level rescue. Wobbler-mouse motor-neuron disease (CNS relevance).
evidence.source: O'Brien 2023 J Cell Biol; Csizmadia 2022 Traffic; Meisler 2008

evidence.claim: FZD7 (TIER 2): Wnt7a->FZD7 activates an AKT/mTOR anabolic input (FZD7 knockdown lowers mTOR + Wnt/PCP activity); FZD7 loss reduces this upstream input but, in TSC2-KO, the dominant RHEB-GTP lock limits the impact. Indirect, moderate.
evidence.source: von Maltzahn 2012 PNAS; Xu 2023 PLoS ONE; He 2025 Front Genet

evidence.claim: RPS3 (TIER 3): a 40S ribosomal protein — a DOWNSTREAM effector of mTORC1-driven ribosome biogenesis, not a regulator. Its loss might signal ribosomal-subunit imbalance via RNF10-dependent ribosome-associated quality control, dampening excess translation in TSC2-KO, but the mTORC1 feedback link is indirect/undefined.

SMARCE1 (TIER 3): a SWI/SNF (BAF) chromatin-remodeller subunit; mTOR pathway is co-targeted therapeutically in SMARCE1-mutant meningioma (associative, not mechanistic). If SMARCE1 regulates chromatin accessibility at mTOR-pathway promoters its loss could suppress the mTORC1 transcriptional programme; also a mitotic bookmarking factor. Circumstantial. Coffin-Siris (CNS).

DPM2 (TIER 3): regulatory subunit of dolichol-phosphate-mannose synthase; DPM2-CDG impairs N-glycosylation. Glycosylation defects could impair lysosomal nutrient-sensing machinery (SLC38A9 etc. need N-glycosylation), reducing mTORC1 lysosomal activation — mechanistically distant but not implausible. CDG CNS phenotype (epilepsy/microcephaly) overlaps TSC.
evidence.source: Radenkovic 2021 Mol Genet Metab; Barone 2012 Ann Neurol

evidence.claim: NCOA6 (TIER 3): a nuclear-receptor coactivator (LXR/ER/CREB); no direct mTOR/autophagy/TSC evidence retrieved. A speculative metabolic/nuclear-receptor link to mTOR-target gene networks; also regulates NLRP3 (mTOR-downstream inflammation). Weak.
evidence.source: Oh 2022 Mol Cells; Lee 2024 Cell Mol Immunol

evidence.claim: SSU72 (TIER 4): an RNA Pol II CTD phosphatase / general transcription regulator; targeted searches returned NO link to mTOR, autophagy or lysosomal function. The screen hit may reflect an uncharacterised transcriptional dependency of TSC2-KO growth. Very weak.
evidence.source: Fidler 2024 Front Mol Biosci; Ganem 2003 EMBO J

evidence.claim: DIDO1 (TIER 4): apoptosis/stem-cell regulator; NO mTOR/autophagy/TSC mechanistic evidence. Only metabolic link is a female-specific BMI/obesity association (mechanism undefined). Likely a false positive or a novel uncharacterised synthetic-rescue. Very weak.
evidence.source: Kaisinger 2023 Cell Genomics; Zhang 2021 Mol Cancer

evidence.claim: SAP18 (TIER 4): a Sin3-HDAC repressor / ASAP-PSAP splicing component; NO direct mTOR/autophagy/lysosome evidence. Could in principle repress mTOR-target transcription or alter splicing of mTOR regulators, but purely speculative. Very weak.
evidence.source: Kumari 2023 Cell Biochem Funct; Zhang 1997 Cell
